issues.title: CI: 'Validate Historical Data' fails -- quirks.json mis-validated against researcher schema (pre-existing)
issues.body:
The **Validate Historical Data** CI check fails with:
```
quirks.json/root: 'researchers' is a required property
```

## Cause
`scripts/validate_historical_data.py` validates the researcher-data files against the researcher-PROFILE schema (which requires a `researchers` property). `quirks.json` is a quirk CATALOGUE, not a researcher-profiles file, so it fails that schema.

## Pre-existing -- does NOT block PR #806
PR #806 (the branch reconcile) did **not** touch `quirks.json` (only `godot/data/balance/defaults.json`). This check fails on `main` independently. So it is inherited debt, not a #806 regression -- #806 is safe to admin-merge (admin override on the inherited red check). Every other check on #806 is green.

## Fix options (~few lines)
- Exclude `quirks.json` from the researcher-profile validation glob, OR
- Give quirks its own schema and route it there, OR
- Move `quirks.json` out of the researcher data dir.

Tier: CI / data hygiene, non-forking (patch any time). NOT Friday-blocking.

issues.title: Reconcile fable/no-emoji-enforcement with main v0.12.0 (#801 cold-open, #789 hiring, ladder split L1)
issues.body:
## What this is

Reconciles the local-only `fable/no-emoji-enforcement` branch with main's v0.12.0 trunk,
ahead of the Friday v0.13 "ladder-breaking league epoch" cut.

**Branch work being landed** (this session): cold-open onboarding #801 (portal shader + F6
capture harness, phone/passcode beat, first-lever advisor nudge), build-vs-ladder version
split (L1 epoch, `ladder_version.txt` SSOT + `get_board_version()`), hiring stitch #789,
incremental enforce-standards + cp1252 codepoint-reporting fix, make-commit wrapper, copy
corpus + design docs (deploy recon, reconcile plan, cat/cosmetics/doom-oracle ideas).

**Approach:** merged `origin/main` INTO the branch (not rebase/cherry-pick) per
`docs/game-design/BRANCH_RECONCILE_PLAN.md` -- one conflict-resolution sitting against a
known conflict set, history preserved for bisect, full gate run before main is touched.
main was 28 commits ahead (v0.12.0 SSOT, 488MB .pck purge #792, blocking ASCII/no-emoji
#751, welcome overlay #783, A/B layout #778, PLAN icons #797); branch was 10 ahead.

## Conflict resolutions (9 content + 1 file-location)

| File | Resolution |
|---|---|
| `game_config.gd` | version = **0.12.0** (main; NO board-key fork), `LADDER_VERSION=1` (branch); unioned welcome_seen/show_hints/ui_layout (main) + play_intros/last_seen_intro_version/get_board_version (branch) |
| `enforce_standards.py` + `.pre-commit-config.yaml` | **BOTH survive**: main's blocking no-emoji hook (#744/#751) AND branch's incremental fast-path + cp1252 codepoint reporting + ladder-aware version sync |
| `main_ui.gd` | unioned main's grouped-hand A/B renderer + branch's cold-open first-lever nudge |
| `settings_menu.gd` | reconstructed play_intros toggle alongside show_hints + ui_layout toggles (each with its own preamble -- naive union would have scrambled the two functions where git factored out the shared row/label setup) |
| `config_confirmation.gd` / `quality-checks.yml` | branch's cold-open intro handoff + ladder-bump advisory heuristic |
| `CLAUDE.md` / `ARCHITECTURE.md` / `DEVELOPERGUIDE.md` | took main (newer), folded stray em-dashes to ASCII |
| `hero-bg-2400w.webp` (+.import) | kept main's `art_source/` relocation (#792); re-pointed cold_open `HERO_ART` to `office_wide_dawn.webp` (in-tree, ships in the .pck) |
| `verification_tracker` / `window_resolver` / `game_manager` | branch semantics + ASCII punctuation (matches main's #751 purge) |

## Gates (run on the merged tree, all green)

- Fast gate: **587 tests, 0 fail** (65/65 files collected)
- Simulation / replay / determinism: **96 tests, 0 fail** (7/7 files)
- scene-nav: pass | no-emoji: pass | `sync_version --check`: 0.12.0 / ladder 1
- .pck guard: `dump_october_31_2025` stays renamed OUT of `godot/` (R100), no >1MB file re-added under `godot/`

## Follow-ups (NOT blocking this merge -- flagged for after)

1. **#789 mentoring-out**: the accept-prompt "mentoring" card should be removed (keep the hiring-screen path), per Pip's ruling. Deliberately skipped here to not delay the reconcile.
2. **Frozen weekly seed**: `get_weekly_seed()` is stuck at `weekly-2026-w0` (ticks-since-boot bug) -- needs the new Friday league seed before the v0.13 epoch cut.
3. **Pre-existing main ASCII debt**: ~16 main-origin docs / art-metadata files still carry non-ASCII (e.g. `WORKSHOP_2_BACKLOG.md`, `L1_CALIBRATION_2026-07-14.md`). NOT touched here (they are main's committed state, not this merge's changes); would surface only if CI runs a full `enforce_standards --check-all`.
4. Cosmetics lane + final portal -> cold-open scene wiring: next-epoch.

**REVIEW BEFORE ADMIN-MERGE.** Do not squash in a way that discards the merge history (bisect value). v0.13 version bump + ladder L1->L2 epoch cut happen as their own release commit after this lands.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Revisit ADR-0014 (conferences): days-granularity + travel-cost fine-tuning + conference data -> pdoom-data

labels.name: league:next
labels.description: Waits for the NEXT league rollover -- forking gameplay, batched to the next epoch

issues.body:
Spun out of #789. ADR-0014 ruled the conference/attendance model in MONTHS of time, but the game now runs at DAYS granularity (ADR-0009) -- so the conference mechanics can be honed considerably. Not urgent; captures the design direction.

## Revisit / add
- **Days-granularity pass.** Re-express conference time cost in days, not months.
- **Per-employee travel cost varies** -- by travel distance, and by budget vs business class, etc. More knobs for the player to fine-tune or DELEGATE (foreseen delegation mechanics: an attendee's trip can be a sink on a manager's Attention, not just the founder's).
- **Lateral build-out.** Conferences reuse the SAME scheduling / commitment / Attention / delegation substrate as hiring (#789). Building the pattern laterally (hiring, conferences, papers) is the game's intended "second layer" of depth -- consider whether that substrate itself deserves an ADR.

## Data
- Conferences are real historical events -- cheap to populate. Plan: **populate the game with data NOW** (keep the mechanics tight), and note as TECH DEBT to later re-package + validate + re-ingest via the **pdoom-data** repo (other forks will want this data; it should not live only in-game long-term).
- Keep juicy fictional flavor (see below) OFF real named conferences -- real names = flavor only; scandals ride fictional/generic venues (see caution below).

## Event space this opens (story / antagonists / later alliances + voting)
Travel mishaps + intrigue: missing luggage, delayed flights, tropical diseases, dodgy stopovers, poolside cloaked strangers, etc. Rich vector for introducing antagonists and (later) alliances / meetings / voting mechanics.

CAUTION: attaching fictional scandal (money laundering, shady strangers) to REAL named conferences/people implies real wrongdoing -- keep antagonist events on fictional or generic venues; use real conference names/dates as neutral flavor only.

Tier: next-epoch / later (not the 48h tier).

issues.title: Procurement + equipment lead-time: pre-order laptops, start-date scheduling, dead-time as opportunity cost

labels.name: league:next
labels.description: Waits for the NEXT league rollover -- forking gameplay, batched to the next epoch

issues.body:
Spun out of #789 (kept OUT of #789's tight cut). An office-economy DEPTH mechanic (Theme B / the "lateral build-out"), not a launch item. Captures a design riff from Pip 2026-07-23.

## The mechanic
Hiring has TWO independent clocks, and the game is aligning them:
- (a) offer-accept -> start-day (candidate notice; scales with relative status, see #789 latency note)
- (b) laptop/equipment order -> delivery (a procurement lead time)

A laptop can be ordered at ANY point in the recruiting process -- order early and it waits, ready for day 1. If equipment arrives AFTER the start day, the new hire sits idle = dead time = wasted potential effort. **Rewards planning / foresight; punishes poor scheduling via opportunity cost.** Opens an inventory-management surface (stock of laptops/desks/etc), tension around committing capital before a hire is certain, and pressure to minimise down-days.

## Design notes (Claude, for when this is built)
1. **Dead-time = OPPORTUNITY COST (lost output), NOT a punitive debuff.** Respects the rage-quit-friction principle; a stacked morale/efficiency penalty double-dips into feel-bad. MaRo/Rams lens: lost-output is decision complexity (plan better), a debuff is interface feel-bad.
2. **Ordering equipment should cost ~0 Attention** (money + a lead-time timer), or a batched "procure N" action. Charging Attention per order taxes the very planning the mechanic rewards.
3. **Equipment = reusable fungible inventory**, not per-hire-committed. A pre-ordered laptop just waits for whoever starts next -- so a fallen-through hire does not punish the planner (worst case: capital tied up). Keeps pre-ordering a safe-ish bet.
4. **Decide when start-day is KNOWN** (certain at accept = solved puzzle, dull; uncertain/revealed-later = the player must gamble order-early-vs-wait). THAT uncertainty is where the tension lives. Keep the underlying value deterministic (seed) for ladder integrity even if not shown.

## Extends naturally to
Desks / floor-space (ties to #791 office economy), compute, other equipment -- a general procurement-and-inventory layer. Same two-timer pattern as hiring, conferences (#803), papers: the lateral scheduling/commitment substrate.

Tier: next-epoch / office-economy depth (NOT the 48h tier). Relates: #789, #791, #803.

issues.title: Early economy: force a small early lease spend; bedroom/basement start hard-caps hires; unlocks small-now, bigger-over-time

labels.name: ship:next-release
labels.description: Next weekly/monthly train; unlabeled = backlog/design-horizon

labels.name: league:next
labels.description: Waits for the NEXT league rollover -- forking gameplay, batched to the next epoch

issues.body:
Progression idea (Pip 2026-07-22): first unlocks should have small immediate strategic impact, growing over time (switching costs). For the office:
- Very early, force a little spend: DECIDE ON A LEASE with starting money.
- Before that: bedroom/basement, SEVERELY capped # of hires (hard base limit).
- Lease unlocks more hires + capability; switching costs later.
- Rent falls due periodically -> predictable AP/cash sink.
Gameplay/progression -> forks the ladder (epoch/next-release). Pointers: finance_engine/ledger (rent as liability/AP sink), hiring cap on GameState, actions/data.

issues.title: Stretch: macOS (Ventura 13.5.2) + Debian/Linux builds for testers (late-flyby or weekend fast-follow)

labels.name: patch:ui
labels.description: Mid-week UI hotpatch -- non-forking, ships any time, does not disturb the live league

issues.body:
Two friends want to play: one on **macOS Ventura 13.5.2**, one on **Debian**. STRETCH for the Friday kickover; acceptable as a weekend fast-follow patch (Pip is fine with that).

## Good news
Godot **cross-exports macOS + Linux FROM the Windows dev machine** -- no Mac needed to build. Requires the macOS + Linux export TEMPLATES installed + export presets added to `export_presets.cfg`.

## THE RISK TO CHECK FIRST (likely blocker)
The Windows build ships `libgodotsteam.windows.template_release.x86_64.dll` + `steam_api64.dll`. **Mac/Linux builds need the platform GodotSteam binaries (`.dylib` / `.so`) OR the game must boot gracefully with Steam absent.** Before attempting a flyby: VERIFY the game does not hard-crash without GodotSteam on Mac/Linux. If it hard-depends on Steam at load, we need the platform libs or a Steam-disabled export variant. (Leaderboard/HTTPS to api.pdoom1.com is cross-platform fine.)

## Platform notes
- **macOS Ventura 13.5.2** is strict: an unsigned/unnotarized `.app` is Gatekeeper-blocked. Friend workaround (like SmartScreen, a bit stricter): **right-click -> Open**, or `xattr -cr PDoom.app` then System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> "Open Anyway". Notarization (Apple Dev acct, ~$99/yr) is later polish, not for a friend-test.
- **Debian/Linux**: standard x86_64 build; friend does `chmod +x` then run. Same GodotSteam `.so` need.
- `tools/build_release.py` is Windows-only -- extend for mac/linux presets or export manually.

Tier: STRETCH / weekend fast-follow. NOT Friday-critical.

issues.title: Launch call: anonymous install ping + remote update check (one request, two jobs)

labels.name: priority:high
labels.description: Important for next release

labels.name: ship:hotpatch-48h
labels.description: Fast-follow 48h after friend links; ladder break acceptable

labels.name: patch:ui
labels.description: Mid-week UI hotpatch -- non-forking, ships any time, does not disturb the live league

issues.body:
# Spec: one launch call that does two jobs

**For: `PipFoweraker/pdoom1`, in tonight's build if at all possible.**
Written 2026-07-22. Self-contained — hand this straight to the build.

## Why this is the one thing worth stopping for

Two problems, one HTTP call:

1. **You cannot count players.** The only game-to-server signal today is a score
   submission, which needs the player to finish a run AND not have opted out.
   `entry_uuid` is per-entry, so it cannot even dedupe people. v0.11.0 shows 135
   asset downloads on GitHub and there is no way to tell how many were humans
   rather than scanners — an install ID is the only thing that answers that.

2. **You cannot tell an existing player that a patch exists.** The game has
   `has_unseen_patch_notes()` in `godot/autoload/game_config.gd:368`, but it
   compares `last_seen_version != CURRENT_VERSION` — **both local**. That shows
   patch notes *after* someone updates; it cannot tell someone on v0.11.0 that
   v0.12.0 shipped. There is no remote version check anywhere in the Godot
   project.

Neither can be retrofitted onto builds already in players' hands. Every cohort
that downloads without this stays uncountable and unreachable **forever**. That
is the whole argument for the 36-hour window.

---

## The call

One request on launch. Fire-and-forget, async, never blocking startup.

### Request

```
POST https://analytics.pdoom1.com/api/event
Content-Type: application/json
User-Agent: <must be set to a real UA string; Plausible drops requests without one>

{
  "name": "Game Launch",
  "domain": "pdoom1.com",
  "url": "app://pdoom1/launch",
  "props": {
    "install_id":   "<uuid-v4>",
    "version":      "0.12.0",
    "os":           "Windows",
    "first_launch": true
  }
}
```

Verified live: the endpoint is up, `/api/health` reports Postgres and ClickHouse
both `ok`, and a test event returned `202`. **No new server code is needed.**

Plausible returns `202` for essentially anything, so a `202` means "accepted",
not "stored" — do not treat it as confirmation. Fire and forget; never retry in a
loop, never block on it.

### `install_id` rules

- UUIDv4, generated **once**, persisted to `user://`.
- **Never** derived from hardware, MAC, disk serial, username or anything else
  about the machine. A random UUID regenerated on reinstall is exactly right —
  it counts installs, which is what we want, and it is not a device fingerprint.
- Regenerating on reinstall is fine and expected. Do not try to make it survive.

### Consent

Gate it behind the existing privacy opt-out (the same one
`godot/autoload/leaderboard_sync.gd:85-91` already honours for score
submission). If the player has opted out, **do not send at all** — not an
anonymised version, nothing.

Reuse the async + durable-outbox pattern already proven in `leaderboard_sync.gd`.
Do not invent a second networking path.

---

## The update check (same launch, second job)

Read the current version from the website, which already publishes it:

```
GET https://pdoom1.com/data/version.json
```

```json
{ "latest_release": { "version": "v0.12.0", "published_at": "..." } }
```

That file is live now, auto-updated by a workflow, and served as static JSON off
DreamHost — no API, no auth, no rate limit worth worrying about.

Compare `latest_release.version` against the local `CURRENT_VERSION`. If the
remote is newer, show a non-blocking notice on the welcome screen:

> **v0.12.0 is available** — you're on v0.11.0. [Get it]

pointing at `https://github.com/PipFoweraker/pdoom1/releases/latest`.

### Rules that matter

- **Never block startup or gameplay on this.** If the request fails, times out,
  or the JSON is malformed, show nothing and carry on silently. An update check
  that can break launch is worse than no update check.
- **Compare semver numerically, not as strings.** `"0.9.0" > "0.11.0"` is true
  for string comparison and would tell every v0.11.0 player they are ahead.
  Parse to integer triples.
- Tolerate the `v` prefix being present or absent on either side.
- Do not auto-download or auto-update. Show a notice and a link; the player
  decides.
- Make it dismissible, and don't re-nag every launch for the same version.

Note this is a plain static-file read with no identifiers attached — it carries
no privacy cost and does not need to sit behind the analytics opt-out. Keep the
two concerns separate in the code even though they happen at the same moment.

---

## Order of value if time is short

1. **`install_id` + launch ping** — un-retrofittable, and the only way to know
   whether those 135 downloads were people.
2. **Update check** — un-retrofittable *for this cohort*: players who install
   v0.12.0 without it can't be told about v0.13.0 either. Every release you ship
   without it extends the problem.
3. Everything else in `GAME_REPO_ASKS_ALPHA.md` (the bug-report panel's false
   "we'll create a GitHub issue soon" message, the `F8`-vs-`N` mismatch, the
   `/contributors` 404) can wait for the fast-follow.

If only one lands tonight, make it #1.

---

## What the website side already does, so you don't duplicate it

- Publishes `/data/version.json` with `latest_release.version`. Live.
- Accepts the launch ping at `analytics.pdoom1.com/api/event`. Live, returns 202.
- Counts arrivals and download clicks via Plausible. **Pip must add a `Download`
  Goal and allow the custom properties in Plausible settings before links go out
  — property allow-listing is not retroactive.**
- `scripts/alpha-watch.py` reads both streams and will start reporting install
  counts as soon as the ping exists.

Nothing on the website blocks any of this.

---

*Filed from `pdoom1-website`. Full spec also at `docs/GAME_SPEC_launch_ping_and_update_check.md` in that repo. Website side is ready and blocks none of this.*

issues.title: Leaderboard: mark runs where dev mode was used (visible badge, NOT exclusion)

labels.name: ship:hotpatch-48h
labels.description: Fast-follow 48h after friend links; ladder break acceptable

labels.name: patch:ui
labels.description: Mid-week UI hotpatch -- non-forking, ships any time, does not disturb the live league

issues.body:
Lightweight transparency marker + filter (Pip 2026-07-22): players should experiment a LOT with the dev tools; we do NOT exclude or auto-reject dev-assisted runs. We MARK a run where dev mode fired > 0 times, and let viewers filter them out on demand. Honest board, zero policing.

## Mechanism
- Set a `dev_mode_used` bool the instant any dev-overlay function is invoked (route dev-overlay mutations through one 'mark this run dev-touched' path -- today dev_mode_overlay.gd pokes state directly, e.g. :279 sets state.doom, bypassing the tracked pipeline).
- Carry it in the score submission + the VerificationTracker export artifact (add the server field versioned-never-silent, ADR-0002).

## Display (both are just client-side reads of the one flag)
- A small marker / colour highlight on flagged leaderboard entries (and the game-over screen). The score STILL posts and ranks -- it just wears a badge.
- A 'Hide dev-mode runs' filter toggle on the leaderboard screen that collapses the board to legit-only runs on demand. Default: show all (badges visible); toggle hides the flagged ones.

## Explicitly NOT this (per Pip)
- No segregation, no exclusion, no auto-rejection.
- Robust replay-verification (re-simulate the recorded log from seed, reject non-reproducing runs) is a SEPARATE, LATER item. For now we trust self-disclosure and keep the barrier to experimentation at zero.

Why: dev functions bypass the action/replay pipeline, so today a dev-assisted score posts with no marker at all. Badge + optional filter make the board honest without gatekeeping.

issues.body: From Pip's v0.12.0 screenshot (2026-07-22): the office floor shows 3 distinct hires (Alex Anderson / Ellis Martinez / Gray Davis) but ALL render as the SAME woman sprite, and the characters are too LARGE relative to the office/surroundings. Researchers carry an appearance_id (body_00/body_03/body_20 etc.) that is not producing distinct sprites here. Two fixes: (1) map appearance_id -> distinct office_floor sprites (variety), (2) scale characters down to sit in the room. Pointers: office_floor.gd, the appearance/portrait pool. Ties to the office-sim pass + the representation/diversity standing rule.

issues.title: In-game music controller (mute/skip/next) + more track variety; expose the doom-triggered rotation

labels.name: patch:ui
labels.description: Mid-week UI hotpatch -- non-forking, ships any time, does not disturb the live league

issues.body:
## Signal (playtester #1, Rick M, after a longer session)

> Music is start to break my mind a little now haha

Note: earlier in the SAME session, unprompted -- "i'm actually digging the music lol". So this is **repetition fatigue over a long sitting, not dislike.** The score works; it just needs an off-switch and more variety.

## Current behaviour
The adaptive score rotates on DOOM-LEVEL thresholds -- so during a stable-doom stretch the same track loops. The only way to force a change today is to dev-mode the doom up (a workaround, and a smell: music shouldn't only rotate via doom).

## Asks (Pip: "seems pretty plug innable")
1. **In-game / pause-menu music controller** -- at minimum a MUTE toggle + a SKIP/NEXT-track button, easily reachable (in-game HUD or pause menu). Cheapest immediate relief.
2. **More track variety** to reduce loop fatigue.
3. (nice-to-have) let the player manually advance/rotate the track, decoupled from doom changes.

## Tier
ship:hotpatch-48h / next-few-days. NON-score (audio only -- does not fork the ladder).
Quick win = the mute/skip control; variety + manual rotation are follow-ons.
Relates to the Fable music workstream.

issues.title: In-game bug reporter (F8) does not transmit -- reports die on player's local disk, confirmation text lies

labels.name: league:v0.13
labels.description: Ships in the Fri v0.13 league/epoch cut (forking-adjacent, defines the epoch)

issues.body:
## Problem

The in-game bug reporter (F8) collects a report and **only saves it to the player's own local disk** (`user://bug_reports/`). Nothing is ever sent to us -- no HTTPRequest, no GitHub API, no backend. For a friends-and-family tester on a remote machine, filed feedback is **unrecoverable** (it sits in their AppData, which they will never open and we can never read).

Worse: the confirmation UI actively tells the tester it *was* transmitted.

## Evidence

- `godot/scripts/ui/bug_report_panel.gd:156` -- Submit calls `bug_reporter.save_report_locally(...)` and nothing else.
- `godot/scripts/core/bug_reporter.gd:124-172` -- `save_report_locally()` writes JSON to `user://bug_reports/` on the local machine.
- `godot/scripts/core/bug_reporter.gd:178` -- `format_for_github()` builds an issue title/body but is **never called** on the submit path. Dead code.
- `godot/scripts/ui/bug_report_panel.gd:182` -- confirmation reads: "Thank you! Your report has been saved. We'll create a GitHub issue soon. Check back for updates!" -- implies transmission that never happens.

## Fix, two tiers

**Tier 1 (5-min honesty patch, ship in the 48h hotpatch batch -- pure UI, does NOT fork the ladder):**
Change the confirmation text to tell the truth: surface the saved file path and instruct the tester to email it to pip@beacongcr.org (or attach it). Stops the silent-void problem immediately.

**Tier 2 (real wiring, next-release grade):**
Actually transmit. Two candidate channels: (a) POST the report to the existing leaderboard backend (api.pdoom1.com) which already has a shipped token; (b) auto-file a GitHub issue via `format_for_github()` + a token. Prefer (a) to avoid shipping a GitHub write token in a public build.

## Context

Surfaced during the v0.12.0 friends-and-family launch (2026-07-22). The pdoom1-website flagged the same concern (see pdoom1-website#147). Tonight's mitigation: the single tester was told to send feedback directly, NOT via F8.

Tier: ship:hotpatch-48h (Tier 1) / ship:next-release (Tier 2)

issues.title: Onboarding: narrative cold-open + first-turn direction (playtest #1: 'lots of info, not a lot of direction')

labels.name: league:v0.13
labels.description: Ships in the Fri v0.13 league/epoch cut (forking-adjacent, defines the epoch)

issues.body:
## Signal (first external playtest of v0.12.0 -- Rick M)

> the main thing i've got so far is there is a lot of information happening immediately and not a lot of direction. If you introduced a bit of a story it could help so there's not as much trial and error just to find out what I'm meant to do. I love the concept though just hard to navigate.

(Also, unprompted: "i'm actually digging the music lol" -- validates the Fable adaptive score.)

## Diagnosis -- two distinct gaps, not one

Rick's "information" and "direction" are separate fixes:
1. **Cold-open overload / no narrative ramp** -- dropped into full game state (many resources, actions, doom meter, feed) with no context or stakes.
2. **No moment-to-moment direction** -- doesn't know what to do FIRST; learns by trial and error.

**#720 already shipped** (first-launch welcome overlay -> Player Guide, + `getting_started_hint` in `main_ui.gd` visible turn<3) and Rick was still lost -- so a dismissible popup + generic hint is insufficient for BOTH gaps. This issue extends #720, it doesn't duplicate it.

## Design (non-score -- does NOT fork the ladder; uses existing assets)

Old-school RPG / Civ II toolbox: hero art + simple text + fade-ins.

**v1 (hotpatch-tier, minimal):**
- **Narrative cold-open:** existing hero art + 3-5 fading text lines; skippable; show-once (REUSE the `games_played` / `last_seen_version` show-once gate already established by #720 / `whats_new_modal.gd`). Establishes world (AI capabilities accelerating) + your role (you run the org trying to prevent doom) + tension (doom rises, limited AP/turns) + **your first task**.
- **First-turn direction:** upgrade `getting_started_hint` from generic to a concrete "do X first" pointer, OR highlight the suggested first action button. THIS is the part that actually kills the trial-and-error -- the intro alone won't.

**Strong unifying idea:** a Civ II-style **advisor/narrator persona** delivers BOTH the context AND the first nudge in one diegetic device ("Doom is rising. Start by hiring a researcher."). One device, both gaps.

**Later beats (splice in whenever):**
- Per-milestone story beats at triggers ("first paper published", "doom crosses 50%") -- narrative that doubles as short-form content.
- Persistent objective line (ties to the `ui_guide_objective` icon stand-in) so mid-game the player always knows what they're optimizing.

## Tier
ship:hotpatch-48h / next-few-days. Non-score. Splice incrementally.

## Open design decisions (Pip's call)
1. Narrator/advisor persona vs plain text crawl?
2. Guided first turn (highlight/force the first action) vs a stronger static hint?

Connects to design principle: easy info-transition / pain-payoff (this is that principle showing up in the wild). Extends #720.
